matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs4988235 (-13910 C>T lactase persistence marker) (LCT/MCM6 enhancer lactase-persistence marker)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an LCT lactase-persistence thesis: the individual may carry regulatory context relevant to adult lactase expression, lactose digestion, diet-response biology, and ancestry-specific enhancer evolution. Symptom interpretation still depends on diet, age, microbiome, and gastrointestinal context.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs4988235 (-13910 C>T lactase persistence marker) dosage suggests this sample carries the LCT prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an LCT lactase-persistence thesis: the individual may carry regulatory context relevant to adult lactase expression, lactose digestion, diet-response biology, and ancestry-specific enhancer evolution. Symptom interpretation still depends on diet, age, microbiome, and gastrointestinal context. This specific marker is curated as LCT/MCM6 enhancer lactase-persistence marker. rs4988235 (-13910 C>T lactase persistence marker) is curated as a LCT marker for Lactase persistence and lactose intolerance context, Intestinal brush-border carbohydrate digestion. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs4988235 (-13910 C>T lactase persistence marker) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the LCT prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an LCT lactase-persistence thesis: the individual may carry regulatory context relevant to adult lactase expression, lactose digestion, diet-response biology, and ancestry-specific enhancer evolution. Symptom interpretation still depends on diet, age, microbiome, and gastrointestinal context. An observed LCT variant places this sample in the curated LCT research context. The local LCT bundle is nutrition-genetics and regulatory-enhancer oriented. Lactase persistence and intolerance interpretation depends on ancestry, genotype, age, diet, symptoms, and non-genetic gastrointestinal context. rs4988235-like upstream enhancer variation is a classic lactase persistence marker in many European-ancestry contexts.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Lactase persistence and lactose intolerance context

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a LCT variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an LCT lactase-persistence thesis: the individual may carry regulatory context relevant to adult lactase expression, lactose digestion, diet-response biology, and ancestry-specific enhancer evolution. Symptom interpretation still depends on diet, age, microbiome, and gastrointestinal context. LCT methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a intestinal lactose hydrolysis, brush-border disaccharidase activity, enhancer regulation, and lactase persistence biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local LCT bundle is nutrition-genetics and regulatory-enhancer oriented. Lactase persistence and intolerance interpretation depends on ancestry, genotype, age, diet, symptoms, and non-genetic gastrointestinal context. rs4988235-like upstream enhancer variation is a classic lactase persistence marker in many European-ancestry contexts.
